headline: US-Israeli strikes kill Iran's Supreme Leader and close Strait of Hormuz, triggering dual chokepoint crisis and global market upheaval

top_stories.title: Operation Epic Fury: US-Israel Launch Joint Campaign Against Iran
top_stories.summary: The United States and Israel launched coordinated military strikes on Iran beginning February 28, targeting leadership, military installations, and nuclear facilities. Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed. 504 sites struck with 1,039 attacks recorded. At least 787 killed. Iran retaliated with Operation True Promise IV, hitting 27 US bases and striking the US Embassy in Riyadh with drones. An interim 3-person leadership council has been formed.
top_stories.investor_impact: Maximum geopolitical risk event. All Middle East assets require immediate reassessment. Oil above $83 with $100+ risk. Defense stocks outperforming. Airlines and transport sectors hardest hit.

top_stories.title: Dual Chokepoint Crisis: Hormuz and Red Sea Simultaneously Closed
top_stories.summary: For the first time in modern history, both Middle East maritime chokepoints — the Strait of Hormuz and Bab el-Mandeb/Suez Canal — are simultaneously closed to commercial shipping. IRGC declared Hormuz closed; Houthis resumed Red Sea attacks. ~30% of global container trade affected. Jebel Ali (15.5M TEU hub) cut off from global ocean trade. VLCC rates hit all-time high of $423K/day.
top_stories.investor_impact: Systemic supply chain disruption. Oil could reach $100+/bbl if prolonged. Europe-Asia routes via Cape of Good Hope add 7-14 days. Container spot rates expected to spike 40-60%. Gulf state economies face severe export constraints.

top_stories.title: Pakistan-Afghanistan Escalation to Open War
top_stories.summary: Pakistan launched Operation Ghazab Lil Haq, striking 46 locations across Afghanistan including Bagram Airfield. Afghanistan counter-attacked, capturing 27 Pakistani military posts. Fighting entered its fifth day. Separately, violent pro-Iran protests across Pakistan killed 24-26. Military deployed under Article 245 with curfews and communications blackouts in Gilgit-Baltistan.
top_stories.investor_impact: Pakistan sovereign bonds under severe pressure. PKR depreciating. IMF program compliance at risk. Regional supply chain disruption in South/Central Asia.

top_stories.title: SCOTUS Strikes Down IEEPA Tariffs; Section 122 Pivot Creates 150-Day Clock
top_stories.summary: The US Supreme Court ruled 6-3 that IEEPA cannot authorize tariffs, invalidating the primary tool used for country-specific tariff escalation since 2025. Trump pivoted within hours to Section 122 with a 15% global tariff, but this authority expires in 150 days (~July 2026) without Congressional approval. Canada retaliating March 4 with 25% on $30B US goods. EU-US trade deal ceiling breached on EUR 4.2B of exports.
top_stories.investor_impact: Fundamental restructuring of US trade policy authority. 150-day clock creates deadline uncertainty. Previous IEEPA tariff refunds potentially owed to importers. US-Canada trade war escalating. US-India deal at 18% provides framework.

top_stories.title: Hezbollah Breaks Ceasefire; Multi-Front Middle East War
top_stories.summary: Hezbollah fired rockets at Israel for the first time in over a year, in retaliation for Khamenei's killing. Israel responded with Beirut strikes killing 52+ and deployed ground forces into south Lebanon. Lebanese cabinet took the historic step of banning Hezbollah military activities. The conflict now spans Iran, Lebanon, Yemen (Houthis), and involves strikes across 7+ Gulf states.
top_stories.investor_impact: Multi-front war raises systemic regional risk. Lebanon reconstruction hopes dashed. Israeli economy strained by simultaneous Gaza, Lebanon, and Iran operations.

regional_summaries.Middle East & North Africa: The most consequential week for the Middle East since the 1979 Iranian Revolution. US-Israeli Operation Epic Fury killed Iran's Supreme Leader and destroyed nuclear facilities. Hormuz closed. Houthis resumed Red Sea attacks creating an unprecedented dual chokepoint crisis. Hezbollah broke ceasefire with Israel. Iranian drones struck US embassies in Riyadh and Kuwait. Gulf states face export paralysis. Fitch warned on regional sovereign ratings. Oil surged 7-8%.
regional_summaries.North America: SCOTUS struck down IEEPA tariff authority in landmark 6-3 ruling. Trump pivoted to Section 122 with 15% global tariff and 150-day expiry. US-Canada trade war escalating with mutual tariffs March 4. US midterm primaries opened in TX, NC, AR. Anti-war protests growing; No Kings movement planned March 28. US economy faces war-driven oil shock complicating Fed rate path. CBO projects $1.9T deficit for FY2026.
regional_summaries.East Asia: KOSPI crashed 7.24% (worst since April 2024) on Iran crisis and North Korea KN-25 deployment. China's military purge continues — 100+ officers removed since 2022, top general Zhang Youxia under investigation. $11.1B Taiwan arms sale stalled ahead of Trump-Xi meeting. Nikkei -3.06%. Japan extremely vulnerable to Hormuz closure (88% oil import dependency).
regional_summaries.South Asia: Pakistan-Afghanistan escalated to declared open war. Pakistan launched Operation Ghazab Lil Haq striking 46 Afghan locations. Domestically, military deployed across Gilgit-Baltistan after 24-26 killed in pro-Iran protests. India navigating US trade deal (18% tariff) and commitment to stop Russian oil purchases. Sensex relatively resilient (-0.44%).
regional_summaries.Europe: European defense spending surged 12.6% to $563B (21% of global). Germany leading at $107B (+18%). NATO Cold Response 2026 exercise began in Norway with 25,000 troops. EU 20th Russia sanctions package delayed over maritime services ban. DAX -3.54%, CAC -1.95%. Energy shock from dual chokepoint closure threatens growth. France cabinet reshuffle.
regional_summaries.Sub-Saharan Africa: US sanctioned Rwanda Defence Force over M23/DRC conflict — significant shift in US-Rwanda relations. South Sudan massacre killed 169 including 90 civilians. Sudan SAF returned to Khartoum but 24.6M face acute hunger. Myanmar military controls only 21% of territory. Ethiopia-Tigray clashes renewed.
regional_summaries.Latin America: Chile inaugurates far-right President Kast March 11. Brazil +0.83% — rare positive as net oil exporter benefits. Venezuela post-Maduro transition continues under Delcy Rodriguez. Mercosur-Singapore FTA entered into force for Uruguay. Argentina peso weakening to 1,414/USD.
regional_summaries.Central Asia: Kazakhstan and Azerbaijan benefit as non-Hormuz oil exporters. Oil price surge provides windfall revenues. Regional stability maintained despite global turmoil.
regional_summaries.Southeast Asia: Strait of Malacca open and functioning normally but may see increased traffic volumes. Thailand hosted Cobra Gold 2026 (30-nation exercise). Vietnam, Philippines economies stable. Myanmar junta continues losing territory.
regional_summaries.Oceania: Australia's ASX -0.5%. LNG export opportunity from Gulf supply disruption. Iron ore prices stable. New Zealand relatively insulated from Middle East crisis.

market_context.summary: Global markets in full risk-off mode driven by Iran conflict and dual chokepoint crisis. Oil surged 7-8% with $100+ projections if Hormuz remains closed. Gold above $5,000 for first time. DXY at 98-99 as dollar weakens. Treasury yields declining on flight to safety. KOSPI circuit breakers triggered.

notable_moves.driver: Hormuz closure; 20% global oil supply at risk
Iran crisis risk-off + NK KN-25 deployment

watchlist_updates.change_this_week: Supreme Leader killed; nuclear facilities struck; Hormuz closed; interim council formed

watchlist_updates.situation: Dual crisis: external war + internal unrest
watchlist_updates.change_this_week: Open war with Afghanistan; military deployed domestically; 24-26 killed in protests
Hezbollah broke ceasefire; Israeli strikes on Beirut; ground forces deployed to south Lebanon
US Embassy in Riyadh struck by Iranian drones; oil exports blocked by Hormuz
Al Minhad struck; Jebel Ali cut off from global trade
Ali al-Salem struck; friendly fire incident; oil exports halted
KOSPI -7.24% circuit breaker; NK KN-25 deployed to frontline
Iran war launched; IEEPA struck down; 15% Section 122 tariff; domestic unrest
9 more PLA lawmakers removed; 100+ total purged; top generals investigated
Hormuz closure halts 20% of global LNG supply from Qatar
Houthi attacks close Bab el-Mandeb; ~$800M/month canal revenue at risk
Best territorial gains since Kursk; IMF $8.1B loan approved; Geneva talks ongoing
SAF returned to Khartoum; 24.6M face acute hunger; 169 killed in South Sudan massacre
Military controls only 21% of territory; 5.2M displaced

data_gaps: Iran economic data reliability severely degraded due to active conflict
Gulf state real-time data limited by communications disruptions
Pakistan Gilgit-Baltistan data gaps due to communications blackout
Some commodity prices (Copper, Iron Ore) dated to January 2026
